title: [CI:BUILD] Packit: run cockpit-podman tests in PRs
body:
See https://cockpit-project.org/blog/tmt-cross-project-testing.html

---

That's what we just talked about in the cabal meeting. This is mostly a foundation for your planned jira card/team discussion, and a good place to collect notes, links, try notifications, etc.

I [tested this on my fork](https://github.com/martinpitt/podman/pull/1). This is [usually green](https://github.com/martinpitt/podman/runs/15525447654). I tested an [extra commit to break the checkpoint API](https://github.com/martinpitt/podman/commit/6a80963bfa11067967b7659c83678f565bb202ba) there, and it [failed the checkpoint tests](https://artifacts.dev.testing-farm.io/161f4a10-d65c-477e-ac7c-3533211b0d9c/), confirming that this really uses the podman package from the PR.

Ironically, the very first run here found an user-visible regression that isn't released yet: containers/crun#1255 .

Questions:
 - Consider not running this on rawhide (too much churn?) - can be changed at any time if/when it becomes too annoying/broken
 - Automatic notification of our team when this fails? That'd be a packit feature, it cannot efficiently be done with GitHub workflows: https://github.com/packit/packit-service/issues/1911 This isn't a blocker for the podman team, as they said they won't even look at our tests much; but it's highly desirable for the cockpit team to pick up regressions quickly

(please feel free to add more)

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
On [fedora-37](https://artifacts.dev.testing-farm.io/1d90d3b7-5f9f-4bbd-9ff3-baf17d18d960/) criu is installed, but [checkpointing fails differently](https://artifacts.dev.testing-farm.io/1d90d3b7-5f9f-4bbd-9ff3-baf17d18d960/work-podman-miscrgx3vnp8/plans/cockpit-podman/podman-misc/execute/data/guest/default-0/test/browser/other-1/data/TestApplication-testCheckpointRestore-fedora-37-127.0.0.1-22-FAIL.png), with "does not support snapshot/restore". This is another real regression, so now it needs some bisecting in the COPR to find the culprit. This is a nice demonstration why I'd really like to know *right away* when a proposed change breaks stuff -- it avoids these long debugging hunts, and helps both our teams.

On Fedora 38/39, criu is missing due to https://github.com/containers/crun/issues/1091 - apparently that bug still happens in the COPR. **Update**: Sent https://github.com/cockpit-project/cockpit-podman/pull/1369 to work around/robustify that. Now it's failing in the same way as F37. Will reproduce locally and investigate tomorrow.
So that checkpoint regression is real, I filed it as https://github.com/containers/crun/issues/1255

> So that checkpoint regression is real, I filed it as [containers/crun#1255](https://github.com/containers/crun/issues/1255)

should be fixed once https://github.com/containers/crun/pull/1256 merges.

EDIT: fix crun PR link

PR LGTM in its current form.

@martinpitt would you mind re-running these tests over a period of few days on this very PR to make the podman team kinda sorta comfortable :smile: . I did something like that for the initial packit enablement: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/17898 . Alternatively, consider adding these to a lower traffic repo where the podman team won't mind that much.

:white_flag: I have a gut feeling that this is maybe too big a step. So I have another proposal:

 - Let's shelve this for a while, and start with cross-project testing between your own projects. These aren't affected by that packit notification feature, and don't have that smell of "we are not familiar with these".
 - Instead, let's start with sorting out your PR vs. main build COPRs, to make test results meaningful and reproducible, see https://github.com/containers/crun/pull/1256#issuecomment-1665695404  ff. See https://github.com/containers/crun/pull/1260 (there will be a similar one for all containers projects)
 - Enable podman's Fedora gating tests in podman upstream PRs. This already tightens the feedback loop in a nice way.
 - Enable podman's tmt tests in crun and container-selinux PRs, and crun's tests in container-selinux PRs., and would have caught https://github.com/containers/crun/issues/1255 right in https://github.com/containers/crun/pull/1000 

That way, your team can become familiar with this, and land changes in these three projects with more confidence.

Then I'll come back to you after a quarter or so, and sort out packit notifications until then?

> white_flag I have a gut feeling that this is maybe too big a step. So I have another proposal:
> 
> * Let's shelve this for a while, and start with cross-project testing between your own projects. These aren't affected by that packit notification feature, and don't have that smell of "we are not familiar with these".
> * Instead, let's start with sorting out your PR vs. main build COPRs, to make test results meaningful and reproducible, see [RPM: include criu dependencies crun#1256 (comment)](https://github.com/containers/crun/pull/1256#issuecomment-1665695404)  ff. I don't immediately see where the bug is, but this can't be right
> * Enable podman's Fedora gating tests in podman upstream PRs. This already tightens the feedback loop in a nice way.
> * Enable podman's tmt tests in crun and container-selinux PRs, and crun's tests in container-selinux PRs., and would have caught [podman from current rhcontainerbot/packit-builds breaks checkpointing crun#1255](https://github.com/containers/crun/issues/1255) right in [Packit: initial enablement crun#1000](https://github.com/containers/crun/pull/1000)
> 
> That way, your team can become familiar with this, and land changes in these three projects with more confidence.
> 
> Then I'll come back to you after a quarter or so, and sort out packit notifications until then?

I'm cool with that. Though I can't say for sure if a quarter will suffice given I'll be getting pushback too :smile: . I can keep you posted.
No-change rebase to current main, as the previous branch was already two weeks old.

Wohoo! You claim the prize of the first project which landed that (I have some others in the works, but they are still in review) :trophy:  . Thanks for your trust for doing this experiment! Please don't hesitate to ping me on questions or issues.

> Wohoo! You claim the prize of the first project which landed that (I have some others in the works, but they are still in review) 🏆 . Thanks for your trust for doing this experiment! 

thanks for this contribution!!

> Please don't hesitate to ping me on questions or issues.

Sure thing. And I'm sure there will be some grumpy pings from our resident Gandalf @edsantiago in the near future :) . So, let's hope the packit feature to ping specific people gets done soon :D

Thanks for bearing with me! Yes, I'm sure we'll hit some bumps here and there, it's a learning experience for both of us. That TF auto-notification feature will surely be very important for scaling this up.
